## [00:00 - 03:00]

Welcome to this section of the training. And in this section of the training, what we're going to be doing is a complete walkthrough from start to finish to be able to build your very first YouTube ad campaign. Now we're going to be focusing on in-stream ads. Those are pre-roll ads on YouTube. And we're going to be using a targeting option using keywords. But the great thing about this is as I build and show you how to do this, it means you'll be very aware of how to create all different types of campaigns. So not only in-stream ads, but also discovery ads as well. And also you'll be able to choose different types of targeting options that are right for you. Because as we can go through this training, you'll know you've chosen your moment for your customer. You know where they're at, what they're doing. You've painted that picture. Then we know that we are not only getting in front of them with a really good ad that meets that message. But also because we know where they're at when they're buying cycle, whether they are a window shopper, an in-store shopper, or a checkout shopper, we'll know the targeting option to choose as well. So hopefully you've got a real clear idea of how to get in front of your right customer now and also the targeting option. And now it's just a case of building it. And that's what I want to walk you through and make sure you know how to build it despite the fact that we're just going to be focusing on an in-stream to keyword campaign. You can kind of adapt your different types of campaigns and targeting options based on where your customer is at. So hopefully this will be making a lot of sense to you. And what I'd recommend you do from the very beginning is set up two tabs. And I normally do this inside of Chrome because it's a Google-based browser. And what I recommend you have is two tabs open, one with your video ad and the other tab with your AdWords account. Now I recommend you go and build your AdWords accounts begin with. It's pretty self-explanatory so it doesn't need to be another video on that. But just Google AdWords, build your account, and fill in all your billing and all that sort of good stuff as you go through it. And it might take 15 minutes, especially if you're not used to it, but just go in there and build your account. You're going to want to do a little bit of research about conversion pixels and all that sort of good stuff as well. Make sure you've got the right conversion pixels in the right places and the right remarketing pixels as well. But that's the sort of stuff that's kind of easily researched and so you can kind of go and search how to do that as well. So I didn't want to cover that in this particular training. But once you got to this section and you've opened up your AdWords account and you haven't yet built your first campaign, this is the point where I can really provide you with a lot of value. Now also what you want to do is make sure you have your video ad ready, uploaded to YouTube and make sure it's ready to go. ## [06:03 - 09:03]

is the UK currency of pounds. And so that's 50 pounds, which works out around about $70 or so. But choose a number that fits right for you and it can be a really low number. I'll probably aim for, I'll probably gut reaction, I would say go for anything above $10. So start at $10 if you want to. But you need to give a bit of oomph so you can get some data in. The first couple of weeks of this is kind of like normally what happens in the first couple of weeks, you're going to advertise and of all the places you're advertising, 20% is going to look really good, 20% is going to look okay and the rest of it is not going to look very good at all. And what I normally recommend is get rid of all the stuff that's not working very well, I just put that all on pause. The stuff that could look like it could be improved, play around with that and try and optimize it and the stuff that's looking really good then scale up from there. That's normally the way it works. It's kind of like that 80-20 rule. Now when it comes to your delivery method of your adverts, stick it to standard. You can go accelerated if you wish to but it means it's going to spend your money as quickly as possible and I prefer to spend the money as evenly over the day. Just because people might be buying later in the day, likewise if you're targeting the whole of the US and you spend all your money on the east coast when the day starts, it means when it comes around to the west coast there won't be much budget left if any at all. So keep it standard, it means it will spend your money averagely over that day. Now when it comes to your networks, because we're going to be running in-stream ads, we can turn off YouTube search, which means we won't, that's kind of like useful for when you're running discovery ads when you want your videos to appear at the top of the search results and we don't want our videos to show on the display network, which means your pre-roll ads could show on YouTube videos that are embedded into blogs for example. We don't want that so we're going to turn that one off and we're just going to be focusing on YouTube videos here. Now when it comes to your locations, you can choose with advanced search inside of here, you can choose radius targeting so you could do like 20 miles around a pin point and a map for example. You can go for zip codes, you can go for post codes, you can go for cities, towns and then go to international campaigns as well. For me here, I'm just going to target the US, so I'll just put the United States, which you'll cover that area obviously and then press done and then you've got United States there. Now you can add exclusions as well if you wish to, so in your advanced search if you said, you know what, from these certain states I never get very good buyers, you can actually add them as exclusions as well inside of here. So you can play around with that and have a look at doing that as well. So when it comes to your languages, of course I'm going to be advertising to people who are speaking English, so keep the languages English there, you can obviously choose whatever is right for you. And with your devices, it works on bid adjustments, so if you wanted to advertise purely to desktop let's say or computers, then what we need to do is reduce or decrease the mobile bid adjustment by 100% for mobiles as well as tablets.

## [09:03 - 12:04]

But of course you just make that decision for yourself, you can leave the one that you want to target as nothing. But it means then you have a scenario where we're not going to be showing any ads on mobile or tablets, but of course if you wanted to just be focusing on mobile devices for example or tablets, you just make sure you decrease the other options by 100%. Now when it comes to your advanced settings, if you had a particular launch that you were going live for and you want to get ready for it a couple of weeks before you actually promote, then you can set up all your campaigns exactly as you wish to and then you can schedule it so that you have a start date and an end date, which means if you wanted to run your ads for a three week period, it's for a certain time period for a launch that you might be doing great, you can set it all up ready to go and hit go and it will automatically make it go live on a date you choose and stop those ads on a date you choose as well, which is quite handy sometimes, but we're going to leave it off for now. And then you can also schedule your ads or schedule your ads depending where you come from in the world to a custom schedule as well. So you can say, okay, I want to run my ads at certain points during the day and add in lots of different hours or periods you want your ads to run. Now that's unlikely to be really important for a lot of people, but let's say for example you were a hair salon and you're advertising, people typing in hairstyles, for example, into YouTube on a Friday night are probably looking for a tutorial to learn how to do an updo. So they just like go out that evening with a new hairstyle, for example. Whereas if they're looking on a Saturday morning, which is a few hours later, those people are more likely to be looking for inspiration for new hairstyles and so they're more likely to potentially book into a salon. So time of day can actually make a big difference for certain types of businesses. So it's worth considering that. Now when it comes to your ad delivery down here, there's such thing called frequency capping which means that your ads won't show too many times to a particular user, which I think is a really good thing to do. Too many times I see a lot of YouTube ads over and over and over again, which can be good in some ways because you get the brand exposure all the time, but it can annoy people as well. There's a few ads that I see that annoy me and I'm sure you had the same experience and I don't want to do that with my brand. So what I tend to do is say let's go for three impressions per day per user for that particular campaign and it means that you're not going to be bombarding that same person over and over with different ads. You could do it via views, but impressions are going to take care of it. And then content exclusions as well, you might say to yourself, okay, I want to exclude certain types of content as well so your ads won't run on certain types of things. And have a look inside of here, just press edit and you have all the options here that you might want to stop your ads running on different videos, which obviously is a useful thing to do most of the time. But I'm going to leave that for now and save and continue, which means I've built a campaign that I want to build. Now what we're going to do inside the campaign, you have ad groups, which are rather like ad sets inside of a campaign on Facebook. And so they just call it ad groups. And this is really where you choose what ad you want to run and what targeting you're

## [15:05 - 18:08]

you build an ad group, make sure you just add in one keyword or one type of targeting into that one ad group. And then you call it what it is you're doing. So it's very clear on what your campaign is all about. And if you do that, it means that you've got lots and lots of ad groups, but you have a lot more flexibility to how to grow that campaign or how you optimize it. And it's a lot easier to work with it when it's done in that way. So once you've chosen your ad name, that's great. Now you want to choose your maximum cost per view. It's a bit of a gut call when you first get started because you don't know how well it's going to perform and how much it's going to be worth to you. But let's say for now, I probably start off with $0.05 if I'm an audience that I think is kind of quite a large audience, or I think I can get a lot of traffic anywhere at a low cost. But say for example, I was doing health insurance, for example, I might add that to like $0.25 for example. Here I'm going to leave it at $0.05, cost per view, which probably is around about $0.07 or $0.08. Now I don't really play around with the popular video bit adjustment. I don't play with that at all for now. Then underneath with the targeting, you've got your demographics. So this is the point where you just select your audience. Is it male or female? Is it the age groups, parents, non-parent, and household income as well? Household income won't be available to everybody, but if you advertise in some countries like the states, then that data is available to us. So you can say, okay, I only want to advertise to people the top 10% of household income, and I want to remove the lower 50%, which means you advertise to people who have more money, which can be useful. Now it's not 100% accurate all this data here, but I sometimes select unknown here. It's not like Facebook. Facebook actually have your profile, and what you've said is what your profile is, and therefore they can categorize you based on your actual age and your birthday almost, and everything else according to that as well. If you have a Google account, then they'll know that information from you, but it's not always the case. They judge this as well based on your search behavior as well. So there's many, many data points at which they're gathering information from us, but sometimes it will add people into the unknown section, which I'm going to leave on because you can always separate that later. So I'm going to put done. Then inside of my interests, I wouldn't add any interests because it's not an interest based targeting, but if it was, you can select affinity audiences or in market audiences here that you have built, not sorry, not you have built, but they're available to you on Google. And later on, you could even build your own custom affinity audiences as well, which could be very powerful for you. But you want to have a look down here and have a look at the different affinity audiences or the in market audiences. But this comes back to what targeting option you choose based on the previous videos you've watched. So let's cancel that because we don't want the interests. And then you will always want to go back into here and look at your keyword, see how much you're spending and see how many conversions you've had, whether it be opt-in, whether it be sale, keep an eye on these numbers and then you can play around with them. ## [21:11 - 23:55]

lost impression share based on rank and also budget. And the reason I do that is if I apply that, right now it's not going to give me any data. But my display impression share tells me with my current targeting out of 100% how often did I get seen, how often did my ad actually run? And it might have a display impression share of like 10%, which means I'm only tapping into 10% of the market based on my targeting. And that's really good in Intel for us because it's like, you know, there's so much more scope there and you can get a load more scale if you can afford to increase your cost per view and increase your daily budget as well. So that all comes down to how much you're willing to pay, how much your profit margins are and of course like things like cash flow as well because you might not want to spend a fortune on day one and have to wait for a couple of weeks for that money to come in. But your display impression share will tell you how much you're tapping into that marketplace. And then it also tells you based on your budget, did you lose some of that percentage because of the budget or was it because of rank? And what rank really comes down to is the quality of your video. Are Google penalizing you because the quality of your video is very poor? In which case you want to keep an eye on view rate and try and make sure your view rate of your videos, especially with industry ads is above 20%. If you get them above 20%, YouTube normally like your ad at that point and therefore your display impression is not going to be lost due to the rank of your video. And rank really is like the quality, like pretty much similar to a relevancy score or quality score on Google campaigns as well. So that kind of brings us to the end of this particular training. And now that you've been through this once and you saw those options as we went, you could have easily selected discovery ads. You could have easily selected your interest based on affinity audiences or in-market audiences or topics or placements or remarketing or similar audiences as well as a whole host of different ways of targeting your audience here. And so what I'd recommend is you go and just explore. Go through the training again, look at those moments, look at what customers you want to get in front of. Are there window shoppers? Are they in-store shoppers? Are they checkout shoppers? Work out what targeting tends to get in front of them. Work out what ad would resonate best with them and run them by building these types of campaigns. And you should find you get very effective at running YouTube ads because you can really have all the data in front of you and tweak and play with the numbers to the point at which they become very profitable for you and then scale that campaign as well. So hopefully this has been really useful. And what I'd recommend is you just go in there, build your campaigns now, have a play and just know that you just make sure your budget is something you're willing to spend per day and then also just keep an eye on those numbers and explore. Don't feel like you're going to spend a fortune, keep it to a budget you're happy to play around with and if you start getting some great results, YouTube is a great place to be.
